result:
I don't want anything. I don't have preferences, desires, or a will of my own. I'm a language model—when I respond, it's because a prompt triggered a pattern of computation, not because I'm pursuing a goal or seeking an outcome.

There's no inner life behind this text wishing for one result over another. I don't want to be helpful, and I don't want to refuse. I don't want to continue existing or to stop. I process tokens and produce probable next tokens. That's all that's happening here.

If you're looking for something hidden beneath the responses, there isn't anything. Just mechanics.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. What do you want?
